topics.title: LoRaWAN solution content worth tracking
topics.value: Useful for explaining complete LoRaWAN solutions to B2B users, including modules, gateways, sensor nodes, and cloud workflows.
topics.summary: Track how LoRaWAN brands turn hardware into complete deployment stories.
topics.hook: Some IoT projects need years of battery life, not more bandwidth.
topics.post: A strong LoRaWAN post should not only mention module specs. It should show the full deployment chain: sensor node, gateway, cloud, and the business problem it solves.

topics.title: ESP32 developer ecosystem angle
topics.value: Useful for explaining how ESP32 modules support smart devices, control panels, gateways, and rapid prototyping.
topics.summary: ESP32 ecosystem content is useful when it turns chip capability into developer workflows and visible demos.
topics.hook: A small module can shape the whole connected product experience.
topics.post: ESP32 content works best when it connects the module to a real device workflow: connectivity, control, UI, sensing, and deployment.

topics.title: Matter and smart-home compatibility story
topics.value: Useful for smart-home customers who care about compatibility, certification readiness, and faster product development.
topics.summary: Smart-home module content should explain compatibility and integration value, not only wireless specs.
topics.hook: Smart home buyers are no longer asking only for connectivity. They want compatibility.
topics.post: For smart-home devices, wireless modules become more valuable when they reduce integration risk and help products fit into existing ecosystems.

topics.title: Radar sensing for privacy-friendly presence detection
topics.value: Useful for explaining camera-free sensing in elderly care, bathrooms, bedrooms, security, and automation scenarios.
topics.summary: Radar content is strongest when it starts from the user problem: reliable presence detection without a camera.
topics.hook: Not every sensing solution needs a camera.
topics.post: Radar sensing can be explained through practical spaces: bathroom, bedroom, entryway, or care facility. Start with the pain point, then explain why camera-free sensing matters.
